name: Jumby Bay Island, an Oetker Collection Hotel

content.verdict: Jumby Bay occupies its own 300-acre private island a seven-minute boat ride from Antigua's mainland — a self-contained world of pristine beaches, no cars (only bicycles and golf carts), and the Oetker Collection's signature understated European luxury. With three white-sand beaches, an Aman-rivalling spa, and one of the Caribbean's highest couples-review percentages, it sits at the very top of the Antigua honeymoon hierarchy.
content.best_room: The Pond Bay Suites are the most coveted accommodations — oceanfront positions with private plunge pools, outdoor showers, and direct beach access. The Estate Rooms in the original plantation-style buildings offer more traditional Caribbean architecture with garden settings. For the ultimate honeymoon stay, the three- and four-bedroom private villas (Sandpiper, Hawksbill, Pelican Beach) come with dedicated staff, private pools, and entire stretches of beach.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Private Island Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into V.C. Bird International Airport and transfer to Jumby Bay's private dock for the seven-minute boat ride to the island. Arrival is met with rum punch and a golf cart tour of the property. Settle into your suite, swim at Pond Bay Beach, and dinner at The Estate House — the restored 230-year-old plantation house that anchors the resort's fine dining.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Beach Day & Hawksbill Turtles
itinerary_7_nights.description: Jumby Bay's three beaches are entirely private. Pasture Bay on the windward side is the nesting ground for endangered hawksbill turtles — the resort's conservation programme is one of the longest-running in the Caribbean. Spend the morning snorkelling, the afternoon with a beach picnic delivered by butler, and watch the sunset from the western shore.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Nelson's Dockyard & English Harbour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Boat back to mainland Antigua and drive south to Nelson's Dockyard — the UNESCO World Heritage Georgian naval dockyard from which Admiral Nelson commanded the British fleet in the 1780s. Lunch at one of the harbourside restaurants, then explore the historic buildings before returning to the island for sunset cocktails.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa Day at Sense
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Sense Spa at Jumby Bay is set in a banyan-tree grove with treatment pavilions opening to private gardens. Couples' signature treatments use local ingredients — Antiguan sea salt, coconut, and aloe. Spend the full day moving between the spa, the adjacent pool, and a long lunch at Verandah, the resort's beachfront all-day restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Shirley Heights Sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Antigua has 365 beaches — one for every day of the year — but only one Shirley Heights. The Sunday-evening sunset gathering at this 18th-century military lookout above English Harbour is one of the Caribbean's iconic experiences: steel band, barbecue, and a 360-degree view down to the yachts in the harbour. Even non-Sunday visits give the same panoramic view.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Stingray City & Sailing
itinerary_7_nights.description: A morning excursion to Stingray City — a shallow sandbar off Antigua's north coast where southern stingrays glide around waist-deep water. After lunch, take one of Jumby Bay's Hobie Cats out into the channel between the island and the mainland, or arrange a private sunset cruise on the resort's catamaran.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Beach Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final morning swim at Pond Bay, breakfast at the suite terrace, and a slow departure back across the channel to the mainland for international connections. The Oetker Collection's seamless service handles every step from villa to airport gate.

caveats: Jumby Bay's prices are at the very top of the Caribbean market — rates from $1,800 in low season to $6,500+ for villa stays in peak weeks reflect its private-island exclusivity.
The seven-minute boat transfer is straightforward but means arrival logistics involve more steps than a beachfront mainland resort.
Jumby Bay is family-welcoming, not adults-only — the resort's scale absorbs families easily, but couples seeking strictly adults environments should consider Hermitage Bay or Galley Bay instead.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Jumby Bay is one of the Caribbean's premier honeymoon properties — the private-island setting, Pond Bay Suites with private plunge pools, the Sense Spa, and the seamless Oetker Collection service create a textbook luxury honeymoon week. Couples consistently rate it among their best Caribbean experiences.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: December through April is Antigua's high season — reliably dry, sunny, and warm. May and June offer slightly lower rates with similar weather. September and October are hurricane season and the resort sometimes closes. February through April is the absolute sweet spot for honeymoons.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: For Christmas, New Year, and February through April peak weeks, book 9-12 months ahead — the Pond Bay Suites and private villas sell out first. Shoulder season offers better availability with 4-6 months lead time.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Jumby Bay is not adults-only — it welcomes families, especially during school holidays. The 300-acre island scale means couples can find quiet beaches and adults-oriented spaces easily, but for a strictly adults environment consider Hermitage Bay, Galley Bay, or Cocobay.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Pond Bay Suites with private plunge pools and direct beach access are the honeymoon sweet spot. For a once-in-a-lifetime stay, the multi-bedroom private villas (Sandpiper, Hawksbill, Pelican Beach) come with dedicated staff and entire private beaches.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to V.C. Bird International Airport (ANU) — direct flights from London, New York, Miami, Atlanta, and Toronto. From the airport, a 15-minute road transfer reaches the private Jumby Bay dock at Beachcomber, where the resort's launch makes the seven-minute crossing to the island.
